IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. 54ff3e88c9e5621412c79c","dweb:/ipfs/QmWZ4rAKTQbNG2HxGs46AcTXShsVytKeLs7CUCdCSv5N7a"]},"lib/openzeppelin-contracts-upgradeable/contracts/utils/StorageSlotUpgradeable.sol":{"keccak256":"0x07ac95acad040f1fb1f6120dd0aa5f702db69446e95f82613721879d30de0908","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://a9df9de7b5da1d1bd3d4b6c073d0174bc4211db60e794a321c8cb5d4eae34685","dweb:/ipfs/QmWe49zj65jayrCe9jZpoWhYUZ1RiwSxyU2s7SBZnMztVy"]}},"version":1}

addSupportedToken(address,bytes32).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Emits TokenSupportAdded event.

params.actionId: The identifier of the proposed action.

constructor.details: Prevents the implementation from being initialized directly.

handleDeposit(address,address,uint256).details: Only callable by WalletRouter. Reverts if paused, token is not supported, or incorrect ETH amount. Emits DepositProcessed event.

handleWithdrawal(address,address,uint256.details: Only callable by WalletRouter. Reverts if paused, token is not supported, or insufficient balance. Emits WithdrawalProcessed event.

initialize(address,address,address).details: Reverts if any address is invalid or WalletRouter is not a contract. Enables ETH support by default.

params.token: The token address to check (address(0) for ETH).

returns._0: True if the token is supported, false otherwise.

pause().details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Inherits from PausableUpgradeable.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

proposeAddToken(address).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Reverts if token is already supported or invalid.

proposeRecoverFunds(address,address,uint.details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Reverts if recipient is invalid.

params.recipient: The address to receive the recovered funds.
params.token: The token address to recover (address(0) for ETH).

proposeRemoveToken(address).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Reverts if token is not supported, has deposits, or has balance.

proposeSetWalletRouter(address).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Reverts if address is invalid.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

recoverFunds(address,address,uint256,byt.details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Emits FundsRecovered event.

params.actionId: The identifier of the proposed action.
params.recipient: The address to receive the recovered funds.
params.token: The token address to recover (address(0) for ETH).

removeSupportedToken(address,bytes32).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Emits TokenSupportRemoved event.

params.actionId: The identifier of the proposed action.

setWalletRouter(address,bytes32).details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Emits WalletRouterSet event.

params.actionId: The identifier of the proposed action.

unpause().details: Only callable by VAULT_ADMIN_ROLE. Inherits from PausableUpgradeable.

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

ADD_TOKEN().notice: Constant for the ADD_TOKEN action identifier used in timelock proposals.

RECOVER_FUNDS().notice: Constant for the RECOVER_FUNDS action identifier used in timelock proposals.

REMOVE_TOKEN().notice: Constant for the REMOVE_TOKEN action identifier used in timelock proposals.

SET_WALLETROUTER().notice: Constant for the SET_WALLETROUTER action identifier used in timelock proposals.

addSupportedToken(address,bytes32).notice: Executes the addition of a supported token after timelock validation.

constructor.notice: Constructor to disable initialization of the implementation contract.

handleDeposit(address,address,uint256).notice: Records a deposit of a supported token for a user.

handleWithdrawal(address,address,uint256.notice: Processes a withdrawal of a supported token to a recipient.

initialize(address,address,address).notice: Initializes the vault with RoleManager, WalletRouter, and Timelock addresses.

isSupportedToken(address).notice: Checks if a token is supported.

proposeAddToken(address).notice: Proposes adding a supported token via the timelock.

proposeRecoverFunds(address,address,uint.notice: Proposes recovering funds from the vault via the timelock.

proposeRemoveToken(address).notice: Proposes removing a supported token via the timelock.

proposeSetWalletRouter(address).notice: Proposes setting a new WalletRouter address via the timelock.

recoverFunds(address,address,uint256,byt.notice: Executes the recovery of funds after timelock validation.

removeSupportedToken(address,bytes32).notice: Executes the removal of a supported token after timelock validation.

roleManager().notice: Address of the RoleManager contract for access control.

setWalletRouter(address,bytes32).notice: Executes the setting of a new WalletRouter address after timelock validation.

supportedTokens(address).notice: Mapping of token addresses to their support status (address(0) for ETH).

timelock().notice: Address of the Timelock contract for delayed execution of sensitive operations.

totalDeposits(address).notice: Mapping of token addresses to their total deposited amounts.

walletRouter().notice: Address of the WalletRouter contract that routes deposits and withdrawals.
